name: Code Mycelial Network Specification
synopsis: Code Mycelial Network (CMN) specification — normative documents, JSON schemas, and conformance test vectors for sovereign code distribution
intent: Provide the complete normative specification for the Code Mycelial Network protocol — seven chapters covering Substrate (discovery & identity), Mycelium (domain manifest), Spore (package format), Taste (safety evaluation), Strain (conventions), URI (addressing), and Algorithm Registry

Ship machine-readable JSON Schema definitions (cmn.json, mycelium.json, spore-core.json, spore.json, taste.json) for validating all protocol artifacts, plus conformance test vectors (merkle, signature, uri, capsule, taste gating, key rotation, bond traversal) so implementers can verify correctness without reading prose
mutations: Unify cmn.json endpoints as typed array — each entry has type/url plus type-specific fields (hash for mycelium, format/delta_url for archive), replacing the fixed-key object structure
Remove mycelium_hash as top-level required field — mycelium hash now lives on the type:mycelium endpoint entry
Relax capsule_entry.required to [uri, key] — taste-only domains can omit mycelium/spore/archive endpoints
Simplify spore dist archive — filename no longer required, resolved via endpoint url template + hash
Add cmn.json as accepted pulse type — synapse treats it as notification, fetches live version from domain to verify

bonds.reason: Uses agent-first-data naming conventions for all field names (e.g., updated_at_epoch_ms, cost_msats, created_at_epoch_s)
